title: CleanSpark Upsizes Convertible Bonds to $1.15B for Expansion
text: CleanSpark upsized its convertible note offering to $1.15 billion, extending a record wave of debt issuance among bitcoin miners seeking to fund power and HPC expansion. The Las Vegas-based company offered zero-coupon convertible bonds due in 2032. After deducting fees and expenses, CleanSpark received roughly $1.13 billion in net proceeds. As part of the transaction the company repurchased 30.6 million shares for about $460 million, with remaining proceeds to be used for power and land acquisition, data-center development, repayment of bitcoin-backed credit lines and general corporate purposes. The transaction was led by Cantor Fitzgerald and BTIG, and the zero-coupon notes carry a 27.5% conversion premium. The large capital raise demonstrates investor confidence in CleanSpark's expansion strategy and AI infrastructure pivot.
